# WiFi Sensing Security — Third-Party References

This folder collects open-source projects related to **security, robustness, and privacy in WiFi sensing systems** for comparison, technical review, and research reference.

## Scope

Projects in this category may cover one or more of the following research areas:

- **Adversarial attacks**: Perturbation-based attacks targeting WiFi CSI classification models
- **CSI spoofing**: Manipulation or injection of Channel State Information signals
- **Privacy leakage**: Identification of privacy risks from passive WiFi sensing (e.g., user tracking, activity inference)
- **Physical-layer attacks**: Security vulnerabilities at the RF and physical layer of WiFi systems
- **Robustness evaluation**: Testing sensing systems against noise, interference, and adversarial conditions
- **Defense methods**: Techniques for detecting or mitigating attacks on WiFi sensing pipelines
- **Secure sensing**: System designs that incorporate security-by-design principles for passive sensing

## Research Motivation

This category directly supports the **security-focused direction** of this repository.

The core research question addressed by this repository includes: *How can WiFi CSI-based fall detection systems be made robust against adversarial manipulation, signal spoofing, and privacy attacks?*

Projects collected here help map the threat landscape, identify open problems, and inform the design of defensive mechanisms in the main research prototype.

## Usage in This Repository

- Literature review and threat model development
- Identifying adversarial attack vectors relevant to healthcare sensing
- Baseline comparison for robustness evaluation
- Informing future federated learning and privacy-preserving extensions

**These projects are not part of the core fall-detection pipeline** unless explicitly stated in the main README.

## License Notes

All projects in this folder remain under their original licenses. See individual project folders or the root [THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md](../../THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md) for details.
